User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Build IO application, capable of being a buyer, a developer, or both.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The unique email address of the user.

displayName.description: The name chosen by the user for public display.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

isDeveloper.description: Indicates if the user has a developer profile and can list websites or offer services.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

DeveloperProfile.title: DeveloperProfile
DeveloperProfile.description: Contains professional details for users who act as developers or sellers on the marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeveloperProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User associated with this developer profile. (Relationship: User 1:1 DeveloperProfile)

bio.description: A short biographical summary or professional description of the developer.

specializationTags.description: A list of tags describing the developer's areas of expertise and specializations.

hourlyRate.description: The developer's hourly rate for custom service engagements, if applicable.

experienceYears.description: Number of years of professional experience as a developer.

githubProfileUrl.description: URL to the developer's GitHub profile.

linkedinProfileUrl.description: URL to the developer's LinkedIn profile.

websiteUrl.description: URL to the developer's personal website or a general portfolio.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the developer, derived from reviews (0.0 to 5.0).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the developer profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the developer profile was last updated.

Technology.title: Technology
Technology.description: Represents a technology or framework used in digital assets, such as programming languages, databases, or libraries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Technology entity.

description.description: A brief description of the technology.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing the technology.

Website.title: Website
Website.description: Represents a digital website asset available for sale in the marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Website entity.

sellerId.description: Reference to the User who is selling this website. This user must have an associated DeveloperProfile. (Relationship: User 1:N Website)

name.description: The display name of the website.

description.description: A detailed description of the website's features, purpose, and benefits.

price.description: The asking price for the website in the marketplace.

demoUrl.description: URL to a live demonstration or preview of the website.

status.description: The current listing status of the website (e.g., 'available', 'pendingSale', 'sold', 'draft', 'archived').

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs to high-resolution screenshots or visual assets of the website.

technologyIds.description: References to the Technology entities used in building this website. (Relationship: Technology N:N Website)

listedAt.description: Timestamp when the website was first listed for sale.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the website listing was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Records a transaction for the purchase of a website asset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

buyerId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order (buyer). (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

websiteId.description: Reference to the Website that was purchased. (Relationship: Website 1:1 Order - conceptually, for a single sale)

sellerId.description: Reference to the User who sold the website (seller). Denormalized from Website for easy lookup. (Relationship: User 1:N Order - from seller perspective)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'refunded', 'disputed').

amount.description: The final amount paid for the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Allows buyers to leave ratings and comments for developers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

developerProfileId.description: Reference to the DeveloperProfile being reviewed. (Relationship: DeveloperProfile 1:N Review)

reviewerId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

rating.description: The star rating given by the reviewer (1 to 5).

comment.description: Optional textual comment from the reviewer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

PortfolioItem.title: PortfolioItem
PortfolioItem.description: Represents a completed project or work sample by a developer, showcased in their portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioItem entity.

developerProfileId.description: Reference to the DeveloperProfile that owns this portfolio item. (Relationship: DeveloperProfile 1:N PortfolioItem)

title.description: The title or name of the portfolio project.

description.description: A detailed description of the project, including its purpose, technologies used, and developer's role.

projectUrl.description: URL to the live project, demo, or relevant website.

repositoryUrl.description: URL to the project's code repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab).

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs to screenshots or visual assets of the portfolio project.

technologyIds.description: References to the Technology entities used in this portfolio project. (Relationship: Technology N:N PortfolioItem)

completionDate.description: The date when the portfolio project was completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores core user profile data. `userId` in the path corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. May include a `telegramUserId` for integration.

Stores developer-specific professional details. `developerId` in the path maps directly to the `User.id` of the developer, also present as the `userId` field within the document. Documents in this collection represent public developer profiles, implying `isDeveloper` is true for the associated user.
Stores globally available technology definitions. Publicly readable by all users.

Publicly viewable marketplace listings for websites (status: 'available', 'pendingSale', 'sold'). Includes denormalized `sellerId` for ownership-based authorization, avoiding `get()` calls. Supports QAPs by only containing publicly listed items.

Private drafts and archived website listings for a specific user (status: 'draft', 'archived'). `userId` in the path refers to the `sellerId` in the document. Achieves Authorization Independence via path-based ownership and denormalized `sellerId`. Supports QAPs for private listings, ensuring only the owner can list their own drafts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user (seller) who owns the draft website.

params.name: websiteId
params.description: The ID of the specific draft website.

definition.description: Records all transactions for website purchases. Includes denormalized `buyerId` and `sellerId` for direct authorization checks and QAP-friendly queries for both parties.

Reviews for a specific developer profile. Includes denormalized `developerProfileId` (from the parent path) and `reviewerId` for authorization independence. All users can read, only `reviewerId` can modify their own review. Supports QAPs for fetching all reviews for a developer.

params.name: developerId
params.description: The ID of the developer profile being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The ID of the specific review.

definition.description: Portfolio items showcasing a specific developer's work. Includes denormalized `developerProfileId` (from the parent path) for authorization independence. All users can read, only the `developerId` can modify their own portfolio items. Supports QAPs for fetching all portfolio items for a developer.

params.name: developerId
params.description: The ID of the developer profile that owns the portfolio item.

params.name: portfolioItemId
params.description: The ID of the specific portfolio item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure robust security rules and efficient data access. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Each document contains all necessary authorization context directly within its fields, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For example:
*   `websites/{websiteId}` documents include `sellerId` to authorize sellers for updates.
*   `users/{userId}/draftWebsites/{websiteId}` documents implicitly authorize the `userId` in the path, which corresponds to the `sellerId` within the document.
*   `orders/{orderId}` documents contain both `buyerId` and `sellerId`, allowing direct authorization checks for participants of a transaction.
*   `developerProfiles/{developerId}/reviews/{reviewId}` and `developerProfiles/{developerId}/portfolioItems/{portfolioItemId}` documents include `developerProfileId` and `reviewerId` (for reviews), ensuring rules can validate ownership/access without cross-document lookups. The `developerId` in the path matches the `developerProfileId` in the document.

This approach ensures that operations like batch writes and transactions remain atomic and do not fail due to complex, cascading `get()` operations, improving reliability and debuggability.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture) for QAPs:**
Crucially, this design separates data based on its security posture to enable Query-as-Permission (QAPs) and avoid rules acting as filters. This is most evident in the handling of `Website` entities:
*   **Public Websites:** `/websites/{websiteId}` is a top-level collection containing only publicly listed websites (`status: 'available', 'pendingSale', 'sold'`). This allows any user to `list` and `get` these documents without security rules needing to filter by `status` or `sellerId` for read access, guaranteeing complete and secure query results.
*   **Private Draft Websites:** `/users/{userId}/draftWebsites/{websiteId}` is a subcollection storing websites in a private state (`status: 'draft', 'archived'`). This ensures that a user can only query and access their own private listings (`request.auth.uid == userId`), making `list` operations secure and explicit. This prevents information leakage and simplifies rules for private content.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private User Data:** `/users/{userId}` and its subcollections (`/users/{userId}/draftWebsites/{websiteId}`) use path-based ownership, directly linking documents to the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **User 1:N & Nested Data:** For entities like `Review` and `PortfolioItem` which belong to a `DeveloperProfile`, a hierarchical structure (`/developerProfiles/{developerId}/reviews/{reviewId}`) is used. This maintains clear ownership and allows for efficient querying of all related sub-entities for a given parent, while `developerProfileId` and `reviewerId` (where applicable) are denormalized into the document for Authorization Independence.
*   **Global Public Data:** Collections like `/technologies` are top-level and publicly readable, making their access rules straightforward.
*   **Shared/Transactional Data:** `/orders/{orderId}` is a top-level collection where access is granted to both `buyerId` and `sellerId` (explicitly denormalized in the document), supporting queries from either party.
